varieties.name: (Common Myrrh, Somali Myrrh, Herabol Myrrh)
varieties.origin: Somalia, Ethiopia, Kenya, Yemen, Oman - Indigenous to Horn of Africa, cultivated for 3,000+ years
varieties.traits: Spiny deciduous shrub/small tree 4-5m tall. Papery silvery-whitish bark peeling in flakes. Green photosynthetic under-bark. Very thorny branches. Grey-green trifoliate leaves 6-44mm long. Compact, drought-adapted form
varieties.yield_notes: Up to 4kg resin per tree annually when mature (3-5 years). Most valuable commercial myrrh species
varieties.availability: Limited in Australia. Specialty online nurseries. May need to source as seeds from international suppliers

varieties.name: (Guggul, Indian Bdellium-tree, Mukul Myrrh)
varieties.origin: Rajasthan and Gujarat, India - Indigenous to Indian subcontinent. Historically used in Ayurveda for 3,000+ years
varieties.traits: Spiny shrub 2-4m tall. Crooked, knotty branches ending in sharp spines. Papery bark peels in flakes. Trifoliate leaves with rhomboid ovate leaflets, serrate near apex. Pubescent young parts
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate resin production. Critically endangered in wild due to over-harvesting. Commercial cultivation established
varieties.availability: Available from Indian nurseries online (Nursery Nisarga, Lalit Enterprise, Paradise Found Nursery). Some Australian succulent specialists may stock

varieties.name: (African Myrrh, Corkwood)
varieties.origin: Widespread sub-Saharan Africa - dry savannahs from West to East Africa
varieties.traits: Spiny deciduous shrub/small tree 3-5m (occasionally 10m). Short bole, dense rounded crown. Deciduous - bare for months in dry period. Peeling bark. Yellow-red flowers
varieties.availability: Very limited. Australian specialist - Herbalistics (herbalistics.com.au) occasionally stocks. Otherwise international seed suppliers

varieties.name: Commiphora erythraea
varieties.origin: Somalia, Kenya, Ethiopia, Southern Arabia - Red Sea coastal regions
varieties.traits: Small tree/shrub up to 5m. Greenish or brownish peeling bark. Trifoliate or 5-7 leaflets
varieties.yield_notes: Good resin production. Valued in perfume industry
varieties.availability: Extremely rare in cultivation. Would need to source from specialized collectors or grow from seed

varieties.name: (Balm of Gilead, Balsam of Mecca, Arabian Balsam)
varieties.origin: Saudi Arabia, Yemen, Oman, Sudan - Historically cultivated in ancient Israel (Ein Gedi, Jericho) from Biblical times
varieties.traits: Shrub/small tree 1-10m depending on microclimate. Rarely spiny. Peeling bark. Pleasant-smelling resin from broken twigs. Pinnate leaves with 3-5 oblong leaflets 5-40mm. Red flowers
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate resin production. Ancient cultivation showed commercial viability
varieties.availability: Extremely rare. Would need specialized sourcing or seed collection

varieties.name: (syn. Commiphora kataf, source of habak hagar)
varieties.origin: East Africa - Somalia, Ethiopia, Kenya. Sometimes sold as sweet myrrh/opoponax
varieties.traits: Small deciduous tree to 6m. Similar growth habit to other Commiphora. Thorny branches
varieties.availability: Very rare in cultivation. Would need specialized sourcing

varieties.name: (Guggul, Indian Bdellium-tree, Mukul Myrrh)
varieties.origin: Rajasthan, Gujarat, Maharashtra, Karnataka - Indigenous to Indian subcontinent. Kerala occurrence: RARE/occasional only
varieties.traits: Spiny shrub 2-4m. Thorny crooked branches. Papery peeling bark. Trifoliate serrate leaves. Gynodioecious flowers
varieties.yield_notes: Plants yield 2-4kg resin over lifetime when tapped properly. Commercial cultivation established in dry regions of India
varieties.availability: Available from Indian nurseries: Nursery Nisarga, Lalit Enterprise, Nature India Nursery, Plantsguru. Ships within India. Typically 12-18 inch plants in medium pots

varieties.name: (syn. Balsamodendron berryi)
varieties.origin: South-West India - Grown as hedge plant in southwestern India including Kerala region. One of only 3 Commiphora species found in India
varieties.traits: Small tree/shrub. Used traditionally as hedging plant. More adapted to Indian conditions than Middle Eastern species
varieties.availability: Rare but occasionally available from south Indian botanical gardens or specialized collectors. May be grown in some Kerala temple/church compounds

varieties.name: (Third Indian Commiphora species)
varieties.origin: Western India - One of three Commiphora species found in India
varieties.traits: Small tree/shrub. Less documented than C. wightii and C. berryi
varieties.availability: Extremely rare in cultivation. No known commercial sources

varieties.name: (Common Myrrh, Somali Myrrh)
varieties.origin: Somalia, Ethiopia, Yemen, Oman - Biblical myrrh. NOT indigenous to India
varieties.traits: Spiny shrub/tree 4-5m. Papery silver-white bark. Very thorny. Trifoliate grey-green leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Up to 4kg per tree annually when mature
varieties.availability: Not available in India commercially. Would need international sourcing or seeds

varieties.name: (Indian Commiphora species documented in literature)
varieties.origin: India - Referenced in Indian botanical literature
